preamble: The shift from the vowel [a] to a rhotic consonant [r] is typically a result of epenthesis (insertion) to resolve hiatus, or a process known as rhoticization (r-coloring), where the vowel acquires rhotic characteristics.

examples.note: Intrusive R: an [r] is inserted after low/central vowels to prevent a vowel clash (hiatus).
Erhua: the addition of a retroflex suffix causes the vowel to rhotacize, blending vowel and consonant.
